purpose: INRC project proposal template (anima CP2 framework instantiation, NOT submitted)
based_on: INRC RFP 3.0 structure (TRDF mirror) + INRC Confluence Join page 2026-05-01 verified flow

anima_draft.title: Cross-substrate Phi measurement for IIT 4.0 consciousness theory: Intel Loihi 3 vs BrainChip AKD1000 vs CLM 170M
anima_draft.pi: TBD (anima needs Korean institutional sponsor - see eligibility section)
anima_draft.institution: TBD (KAIST CCN / SNU EE / POSTECH AI candidates)

proposal_structure_10section.title: Executive summary (200 words)
proposal_structure_10section.anima_draft_text: anima is a research framework for empirically measuring consciousness via Integrated Information Theory 4.0 (Tononi et al. 2023). The CP2 (Consciousness Paradigm 2) variant operationalizes Phi as a sample-partition covariance log-determinant computed over hidden-state representations of an information-processing substrate. The framework has been validated on a 170M-parameter language model running on H100 GPUs and an EEG -> AKD1000 spike-event pipeline. The proposed Loihi 3 study extends this to Intel's frontier neuromorphic hardware as a second neuromorphic measurement axis, providing the first cross-neuromorphic empirical test of Putnam's (1967) multi-realizability hypothesis applied to information-integration measures of consciousness.

proposal_structure_10section.title: Background and motivation
key_references: AKD1000 N-3 result (if available by submission)

proposal_structure_10section.title: Specific aims (3-5)
aims: Aim 1: Phi measurement on Loihi 3 spike trains using anima_phi_v3 substrate-agnostic formulation
Aim 2: Loihi 3 vs AKD1000 Phi correlation (cross-neuromorphic Putnam test)
Aim 3: Loihi 3 vs CLM 170M GPU Phi correlation (cross-substrate Putnam extension)
Aim 4: graded-spike (32-bit) vs binary-spike Phi contribution analysis (bit-precision invariance test)
Aim 5: on-chip STDP / 3-factor learning ablation effect on Phi (plasticity coupling test)

proposal_structure_10section.title: Loihi-specific technical plan
components: Lava Process graph SNN model (input encoder + LIF/graded layers + output spike monitor)
16-prompt fixture port from N-3 (re-use anima_phi_v3 PROMPTS list)
T=200ms inference window per prompt, 40 reps for averaging
Spike rate-avg projection: X_loihi[i,d] = (1/T) * sum_t S[t,d]
anima_phi_v3 input contract: X_loihi (16 x D_loihi) -> sample-partition cov-logdet -> Phi*
NRC vLab compute budget: ~200 hr / 6 mo conservative

milestones.M1: NRC onboarding + Lava SDK + smoke run
milestones.M2: Lava Process graph + spike-rate avg helper authored
milestones.M5: Loihi 3 vs CLM r + graded vs binary ablation
milestones.M6: On-chip learning ablation + final report draft + arXiv preprint

proposal_structure_10section.title: Innovation and impact
innovations: First cross-neuromorphic Phi comparison study (graded-spike Loihi 3 vs binary-spike AKD1000)
First IIT 4.0 Phi measurement on Loihi 3 graded spikes
First on-chip learning ablation effect on Phi

proposal_structure_10section.title: Personnel and qualifications
proposal_structure_10section.anima_status: PI bio + anima collaborator role + Korean co-PI institution credentials

request.reason_no_funding: anima self-funded; INRC membership is free per RFP

proposal_structure_10section.title: Bibliography
key_refs: Tononi G et al. (2023) Integrated Information Theory 4.0
Davies M et al. (2018) Loihi: A Neuromorphic Manycore Processor with On-Chip Learning
Intel Labs (2021) Loihi 2 + Lava framework press release
anima paradigm v11 internal citation (TBD external publication)

submission_email_draft_template.subject_template: INRC Project Proposal Submission: Cross-substrate Phi measurement for IIT 4.0 (Loihi 3 vs AKD1000 vs CLM)
body_template_paragraphs: Para 1: PI name + institution + brief intro (1-2 sentences)
Para 3: Loihi 3 (or Loihi 2 fallback) hardware request + estimated NRC compute hours
Para 5: Request RFP + project proposal template; intent to submit per template within 3 weeks
Para 6: Korean institution legal counsel availability for Participation Agreement review
Sign-off: PI name + title + institution + contact
attachment_recommendations: anima paradigm v11 brief abstract (1 page, optional)

what_NOT_to_include: DO NOT include CP2 internal terminology without definition (jargon ratio <= 0.30)
DO NOT cite anima internal docs as primary refs (use peer-reviewed external where possible)
DO NOT request hardware loaner in initial application (escalate after cloud-only path validated)
DO NOT mention $0 budget as a benefit (Intel doesn't care about applicant cost; care about scientific value)
DO NOT promise IP transfer to Intel; Participation Agreement standard terms negotiable on IP retention
out_of_scope_disclaimer: This template is a DRAFT for future user-led submission. anima agent does NOT send this email. anima agent does NOT contact Intel personnel. anima agent does NOT create accounts on intel-ncl.atlassian.net or NRC vLab. All submission actions require explicit user decision and Korean institutional sponsor.
